source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in Fig. 2.32, an infinite wire carries a current $I$ in the $+z$ direction. A rectangular loop of wire of side $l$ is connected to a voltmeter and moves with velocity $\mathbf{u}$ radially away from the wire. Indicate which terminal ( $a$ or $b$ ) of the voltmeter is positive.
extraction_notes: image entry is a PIL object string representation, not a filesystem path or URL

candidate_problem_record.subject: 物理
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: As shown in Fig. 2.32, an infinite wire carries a current $I$ in the $+z$ direction. A rectangular loop of wire of side $l$ is connected to a voltmeter and moves with velocity $\mathbf{u}$ radially away from the wire. candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: image entry is a PIL object string representation, not a filesystem path or URL
metadata.reasoning_chain: The magnetic field at a point of radial distance $r$ is $B=\frac{\mu_{0} I}{2 \pi r}$ and its direction is perpendicular to and pointing into the paper. Note that $\mathbf{u} \times \mathbf{B}$ is in the $+z$ direction. As $V>0$, terminal $a$ is positive.
